data.title: Aave founder: A proposal has been made to launch a new Aave staking module to enable AAVE repurchase and fee conversion
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Aave founder Stani.eth posted on the X platform that the community has initiated a proposal to launch a new Aave staking module to achieve AAVE repurchase and fee conversion.

data.title: ZKsync: During the protocol upgrade v26, there was a temporary problem with the Mailbox contract. It is recommended to migrate to BridgeHu
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News ZKsync issued a reminder on the X platform that during the protocol upgrade v26, there was a temporary problem with the backward compatibility of the Mailbox contract, which would affect L1 → L2 transactions. L1 → L2 transactions were originally handled by the Mailbox contract, but during the v26 upgrade, because the bridge and L2 were in different transition stages, backward compatibility did not work temporarily. 
It may take 1.5-2 weeks to solve this problem, so users are advised to use "requestL2TransactionDirect requestL2TransactionTwoBridges" on BridgeHub instead of Mailbox, and it is recommended to migrate to BridgeHub to avoid the Mailbox contract being deprecated in the future.

data.title: ZKsync Advises Users on Protocol Upgrade Compatibility Issues
data.description: According to Odaily, ZKsync has issued a notice on the X platform regarding a temporary compatibility issue during the protocol upgrade to version 26. The Mailbox contract, which initially handles L1 to L2 transactions, is experiencing backward compatibility problems due to the different transitional stages of bridging and L2 during the upgrade. This issue may take approximately 1.5 to 2 weeks to resolve. Users are advised to use 'requestL2TransactionDirect' and 'requestL2TransactionTwoBridges' on BridgeHub instead of Mailbox. Additionally, ZKsync recommends migrating to BridgeHub to avoid potential deprecation of the Mailbox contract in the future.

data.title: CoreWeave may go public with an IPO valuation of $30 billion
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the disclosed IPO documents, cloud computing provider CoreWeave may go public with an IPO valuation of $30 billion. It is reported that CoreWeave and Bitcoin mining company Core Scientific plan to invest $1.2 billion to expand the data center in Texas. The two parties have also reached a 12-year AI data center expansion agreement. (The Information)

data.title: BitMart will list Bedrock(BR)
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch Bedrock (BR) at 22:00 (Eastern Time) on March 20, and the BR/USDT trading pair will be opened. BR is Bedrock's native governance token and forms the basis of its Proof of Stake Liquidity (PoSL) system, a framework that combines liquidity, governance and sustainable rewards.

data.title: Tether Emerges as Major U.S. Treasury Buyer
data.description: According to Odaily, Tether has become the seventh-largest holder of U.S. Treasury securities, with holdings amounting to $33.1 billion. This positions Tether alongside countries like the United Kingdom and Canada in terms of U.S. Treasury ownership.

data.title: Infini Labs Accuses Engineer of Embezzling $49.5 Million USDC
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Infini Labs has filed a lawsuit against Chen Shanxuan, an engineer accused of embezzling approximately $49.5 million USDC. The lawsuit was submitted by Infini Labs' Hong Kong subsidiary, BP SG Investment Holding Limited, alleging that Chen, who was a lead developer, retained 'super admin' privileges without informing the team. 

In late February, a significant amount of funds was transferred without the required multi-signature authorization. Evidence presented by the plaintiff suggests that Chen has a severe gambling habit, which may have driven him to commit the alleged theft due to substantial debts. Prior to the incident, Chen reportedly borrowed money from various individuals and engaged with underground money lenders.

The company has requested the court to freeze the relevant assets and compel the suspect to disclose his identity and transaction details.

data.title: The number of initial jobless claims in the United States for the week ending March 15 was 223,000, compared with expectations of 224,000 and a previous value of 221,000.
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The number of initial unemployment claims in the United States for the week ending March 15 was 223,000, with an expected number of 224,000, and the previous value was revised from 220,000 to 221,000.
The number of people who continued to apply for unemployment benefits in the United States for the week ending March 8 was 1.892 million, with an expected number of 1.887 million, and the previous value was revised from 1.87 million to 1.859 million.
The four-week average of initial unemployment claims in the United States for the week ending March 15 was 227,000, and the previous value was revised from 226,000 to 226,250. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Weekly Jobless Claims Slightly Lower Than Expected
data.description: According to PANews, the number of initial jobless claims in the United States for the week ending March 15 was reported at 223,000. This figure is slightly below the anticipated 224,000 claims. The previous week's data was revised from 220,000 to 221,000.

data.title: Analyst PlanB Comments on Current Market Conditions
data.description: According to BlockBeats, renowned analyst PlanB recently stated on social media that a genuine bull market has not yet emerged.
